political.s: Delta gave $1,004,500 to federal candidates in 2023-2024, with contributions split bipartisan (32% Republican, 57% Democratic). The company spent $4.3-5.6 million on lobbying in 2024, focusing on aviation industry issues. FEC data shows a 56% Republican allocation which aligns with industry advocacy priorities.

charity.s: Delta contributed $63 million to charities in 2024 through Delta Air Lines and The Delta Air Lines Foundation, returning 1% of annual net income to communities. Primary focus areas include environment, equity, education, and wellness, with major partnerships in blood drives, cancer research, and disaster relief.
charity.focus: Environment, Education, Equity, Wellness, Disaster Relief

environment.s: Delta committed to net-zero carbon emissions by 2050 and saved 45 million gallons of jet fuel through operational efficiency. The company increased sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) procurement to 23 million gallons in 2025 and achieved 28% greater fuel efficiency in new aircraft. Absolute emissions rose 23% in 2023 due to increased air travel demand.

labor.s: Only 20% of Delta's 100,000 workforce is unionized, compared to 82-87% at competitors. Active organizing campaigns exist for flight attendants and ramp workers under the Railway Labor Act. Delta maintains an open-door policy and competitive compensation, though workers cite concerns about scheduling flexibility and safety protocols.

dei.s: Delta actively maintains DEI initiatives despite Trump administration pressure, reframing them as 'people initiatives' focused on talent. The company conducted racial equity training for 80% of officers and senior leaders, and established 12 Business Resource Groups with 35,000 members. CEO committed to doubling Black officers and directors by 2025.

privacy.s: Delta implements 'privacy by design' approach with comprehensive data protection policies and safeguards. The company conducts privacy impact assessments for new processes and maintains 40 Privacy Champions across the organization. However, Delta reserves the right to alter privacy policies without prior notice.

execPay.s: CEO Ed Bastian received $19.2 million in 2025 total compensation ($1.08M base + $11.91M stock), representing a 336:1 CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio in 2023. Over 80% of compensation is performance-based. His pay declined from $34.2 million in 2023 but remains the highest among airline peers.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
